scenarios.reasoning: AIX 7.2 standard support ended April 2023. The OS is currently in Extended Support (through April 2028). Primary trigger 'Operating System is nearing End-of-Life (EOL)' is met. Security patch coverage is reduced under Extended Support terms.
matched_triggers: Operating System no longer receiving security patches under Standard Support

scenarios.scenario_name: Switch to standard Linux Operating System
scenarios.reasoning: AIX is explicitly listed as a commercial proprietary non-Linux Unix system in scenario triggers. Migrating to RHEL or Ubuntu would reduce licensing overhead, improve tooling, and increase cloud compatibility. COBOL can run on Linux via GnuCOBOL or IBM Open XL COBOL.
matched_triggers: Operating System is a commercial or proprietary non-Linux system (e.g. AIX, HP-UX, Solaris, IRIX)

scenarios.reasoning: Application runs on IBM POWER/AIX architecture, not x86/x64. AIX is a proprietary Unix OS explicitly excluded by the scenario. COBOL-2014 on AIX is a proprietary closed-source runtime incompatible with ARM migration path.
matched_exclusions: Legacy Windows or proprietary Unix OS

scenarios.reasoning: No application server exists. The 1-Tier COBOL architecture bypasses any application server layer; the application accesses Oracle directly.

scenarios.scenario_name: Application Migration to Cloud Infrastructure (Lift & Shift)
scenarios.reasoning: On-Premise deployment directly triggers this scenario. Cloud migration of COBOL workloads is technically feasible (e.g., IBM Cloud, AWS Mainframe Modernization). The AIX dependency and 1-Tier architecture increase migration complexity but do not block cloud hosting.

AIX (legacy proprietary Unix OS) is explicitly excluded from containerization. The COBOL monolith is not container-ready. Containerization requires Linux migration and application decomposition as prerequisites.

Although the 1-Tier monolithic architecture and zero API endpoints are strong refactoring triggers, the planned decommission date of 2027 (within 6-18 months of analysis date 2026-07-02) matches the exclusion criterion 'Application is already planned for decommissioning'. A large refactoring investment is not economically justified before decommission.
matched_exclusions: Application is already planned for decommissioning

scenarios.reasoning: Oracle 19c Premier Support ended January 2024. As of July 2026, the database is in Extended Support with only ~6 months remaining before Extended Support also ends (January 2027). This creates immediate urgency for an upgrade or migration.
matched_triggers: Database Support is End of Life / Outdated
Database version is no longer patched under Premier Support

scenarios.scenario_name: Switch DB Engine to open-source database solution
scenarios.reasoning: Oracle 19c is a primary trigger for this scenario. DB License required = Yes confirms commercial licensing costs. The application is custom-developed, providing flexibility to migrate to PostgreSQL. Open-source migration reduces long-term license expenditure significantly.
matched_triggers: License Cost is for database is High

scenarios.reasoning: All three technology components (AIX 7.2, COBOL-2014, Oracle 19c) are classified as OUTDATED. COBOL-2014 is a legacy language standard with shrinking developer ecosystem. The full stack has end-of-life risk indicators from vendors and community.
matched_triggers: Used Programming language is legacy or outdated
Application has End-of-Life (EOL) indicators from vendor or community
